tldr: Inference notebook for a machine translation competition using a pre-trained ByT5 model with beam search, supplemented by commented-out rule-based translation memory and length-matching post-processing steps.
summary: This notebook provides an inference pipeline for the Deep Past Initiative machine translation competition. It loads a pre-trained ByT5 sequence-to-sequence model and runs inference on test data using a custom PyTorch Dataset and DataLoader. The generation uses beam search (4 beams) with early stopping and a max length of 512. The author also includes commented-out code for a rule-based baseline that builds a translation memory bank and bidirectional confidence dictionary using TF-IDF and Dice coefficients, along with a length-matching post-processing step called SentenceAlign to merge predictions.
extensive_summary:
## Inference Pipeline
The notebook implements a baseline inference script for the Deep Past Initiative – Machine Translation competition. It loads a pre-trained `byt5-akkadian-model` using `transformers.AutoTokenizer` and `AutoModelForSeq2SeqLM`. The device is set to CUDA if available. Test data is loaded from a CSV containing `transliteration` and `id` columns.

## Data Preparation & Tokenization
A custom `InferenceDataset` wraps the test DataFrame. Each source text is prefixed with `"translate Akkadian to English: "`. Tokenization uses `max_length=512`, `padding="max_length"`, and `truncation=True`, returning PyTorch tensors. A `DataLoader` processes batches of size 16 without shuffling.

## Inference & Generation
The model runs in evaluation mode (`model.eval()`). Inference uses `torch.no_grad()` and `model.generate()` with `num_beams=4`, `early_stopping=True`, and `max_length=512`. Outputs are batch-decoded with `skip_special_tokens=True`, stripped of whitespace, and collected. Empty predictions are replaced with `"broken text"`.

## Submission
Predictions are saved to `submission.csv` with columns `id` and `translation`.

## Commented Baseline & Post-Processing
The notebook also contains commented-out code for two additional components. First, a rule-based TR-TRY baseline that constructs a translation memory bank and bidirectional confidence dictionary from the training set. It normalizes source text by removing subscripts, brackets, and special characters, then builds TF-IDF vectors (char n-grams 3-6, word n-grams 1-2). Retrieval combines character and word TF-IDF scores with weights (0.75, 0.20), applies a length penalty, reranks with `SequenceMatcher`, and falls back to a Dice-coefficient-based word dictionary. Second, a SentenceAlign post-processing step that compares the model's output length with the rule-based output, appending the remaining text from the rule-based prediction to the model's output at the nearest whitespace boundary if the source is long enough, and appending a period if missing.
post_processing: Replaces empty predictions with "broken text"; commented code includes whitespace-aligned text merging and period appending.
notable_techniques: Beam search generation with early stopping
TF-IDF retrieval with length penalty and SequenceMatcher reranking
